incident.name: Garden Grove MMA Tank Leak
incident.status_headline: OCFA conducting an all-night mission on May 24–25 to confirm if the BLEVE threat from the GKN Aerospace MMA tank has been eliminated after multiple cracks were discovered; ~50,000 residents remain under evacuation orders pending a Monday update.

evacuation.boundary_text: North of Trask Avenue, south of Ball Road, east of Valley View Street, west of Dale Street

official_statements.text: We're gonna be conducting a mission tonight. It'll be an all-night mission in order to confirm that the pressure in the tank has been released and that the BLEVE threat is eliminated. The BLEVE threat is the worst-case catastrophic event that we have been talking about. We are not there yet. We need to run this operation tonight.

Firefighters gathered 'positive intel' overnight during a specialized reconnaissance operation. Crews identified what appeared to be a potential crack in the tank that may be relieving some internal pressure.
Internal temperature of the tank had risen from 77 degrees to 90 degrees. Crews remain focused on preventing either of the two scenarios: a rupture and chemical spill or an explosion.

There are literally two options left remaining: One, the tank fails and spills a total of about 6,000 to 7,000 gallons of very bad chemicals into the parking lot and that area. Or two, the tank goes into a thermal runaway and blows up, affecting the tanks that are around it that have fuel or chemicals in them as well.

official_statements.agency: OCFA (Interim Chief TJ McGovern) — Sunday evening update
official_statements.text: Officials confirmed multiple cracks have been discovered in the unstable chemical tank at GKN Aerospace, not just one.

official_statements.agency: FOX 11 (field report citing tank gauge reading)
official_statements.text: The failing toxic chemical tank is now above the 100°F threshold on its gauge. The Orange County Fire Authority warns that the high temps signal that the risk of catastrophic failure or explosion that was feared earlier in the weekend remains.
The safety of Orange County residents is the top priority.

official_statements.agency: Orange County District Attorney Todd Spitzer
official_statements.text: We will be looking and conducting a full investigation to determine whether or not there's been incidents in the past, and whether or not this particular company has been derelict in its duties to protect the public.

official_statements.agency: City of Garden Grove (GG Alert — most recent banner)
official_statements.text: Evacuation orders have been lifted for all residential areas. Returning our community safely to their homes is always a top priority. Please continue to avoid the closed areas.
